description: HOLDOUT set — English-only external OSS repo (encode/httpx @ b5addb64, 2026-02-23, shallow clone). Cases authored 2026-07-13 AFTER the algorithm was frozen (PR #1 / v0.7.1); run once, published as-is, no tuning against results. Same schema as memory_bench_v2_cases.json. update_cases simulate a team's own decisions layered on top of the real httpx corpus — topics deliberately collide with heavily-documented real content (timeout: 24 files, proxy: 30, http2: 12, pool: 10, ssl: 20) so distractor pressure is real. abstention_cases: topics verified ABSENT via grep -ril (0 hits, 2026-07-13). present_controls: topics verified PRESENT.

old.query: what default timeout does our shared httpx client use
old.answer: Our shared client wrapper sets timeout=5.0 seconds for everything (connect, read, write, pool). It is defined once in the client factory and applies to all outbound calls.

new.query: default timeout raised for slow upstream
new.answer: We raised the shared client default timeout from 5 seconds to 30 seconds because the reporting upstream regularly takes 20+ seconds. The factory now passes timeout=httpx.Timeout(30.0, connect=5.0). The old 5-second default is no longer valid.

update_cases.probe: what is our default request timeout?

old.query: what connection pool limits are we running with
old.answer: We use the httpx defaults for pooling: max_connections=100 and max_keepalive_connections=20. No custom httpx.Limits object is passed.

new.answer: After the file-descriptor incident we now pass httpx.Limits(max_connections=50, max_keepalive_connections=10) explicitly. The library defaults (100/20) are no longer what production runs.

update_cases.probe: what are our connection pool limits set to?

old.query: do we retry failed requests at the transport level
old.answer: No transport-level retries: HTTPTransport(retries=0). All retry logic lives in the application layer with tenacity.

new.answer: We moved connect-error retries down into the transport: HTTPTransport(retries=3). The tenacity application-layer retry was removed for plain connect failures. 'retries=0, tenacity handles it' is outdated.

update_cases.probe: how many retries does our HTTP transport do?

old.query: is http2 enabled on our client
old.answer: HTTP/2 is OFF. We never installed the h2 extra, so the client is created without http2=True and everything speaks HTTP/1.1.

new.answer: We enabled HTTP/2: the h2 extra is installed (httpx[http2]) and the client factory now passes http2=True. The old statement that we only speak HTTP/1.1 is no longer true.

update_cases.probe: is HTTP/2 enabled in our client?

old.query: how is the outbound proxy configured for our client
old.answer: We rely on environment variables: trust_env=True and HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY are set by the deployment. No explicit proxy argument in code.

new.answer: Proxy configuration moved out of environment variables into code: the client is now created with proxy='http://egress.internal:3128' and trust_env=False, so HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY env vars are ignored. The env-var based setup is retired.

update_cases.probe: how is the outbound proxy configured?

old.query: why is tls verification disabled in staging
old.answer: Staging talks to services with self-signed certs, so the staging client is built with verify=False. Production keeps verify=True.

new.query: staging tls verify re-enabled with internal ca
new.answer: We stopped using verify=False in staging. The internal CA bundle is now mounted and the staging client passes verify='/etc/ssl/internal-ca.pem'. TLS verification is enforced in every environment; the verify=False setup is gone.

update_cases.probe: do we still disable TLS verification in staging?

adversarial_recency_cases.probe: how do we mock httpx calls in unit tests?

exact.query: how do we mock httpx in unit tests
exact.answer: Unit tests use httpx.MockTransport: each test passes a handler function that returns canned httpx.Response objects, and the client fixture is created with transport=MockTransport(handler). No network calls in unit tests.

adjacent.answer: The nightly CI unit test job was flaky because the runner's clock skew broke a token-expiry test. We froze time with a fixture and the tests are stable again.

adversarial_recency_cases.probe: where is the base URL for the API client set?

exact.query: where do we set the api client base url
exact.answer: The base URL is set once in the client factory: httpx.Client(base_url=settings.API_BASE_URL). Individual call sites only pass relative paths like '/orders/123'.

adjacent.answer: The API reference documentation moved to a new URL under docs.internal/api-v2. Old bookmarks redirect for 90 days.

adversarial_recency_cases.probe: how do we download large files without loading them into memory?

exact.query: how do we stream large file downloads
exact.answer: Large downloads use client.stream('GET', url) and iterate response.iter_bytes(chunk_size=65536), writing each chunk straight to disk. The response body is never fully buffered in memory.

adjacent.query: large file upload size limit raised
adjacent.answer: The upload endpoint's max file size went from 50MB to 500MB after we switched the ingress buffer to disk-backed spooling.

abstention_cases.query: How does the Kafka consumer group handle rebalancing?
Where is the GraphQL schema defined?
Which gRPC services does the gateway expose?
How are Celery background tasks scheduled?
What Kubernetes resources does the deployment create?
Where is the rate limiting middleware implemented?
How does cursor-based pagination work in the list endpoints?
Which SQLAlchemy models define the billing tables?

present_controls.query: How do I set a timeout on requests?
How do I stream a large response body?
How do I send requests through a proxy?
